arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line dotnet 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5; echo "---"; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i binutils | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try fetching debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using the Build ID
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"

# Try the ELF debug symbol endpoint
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try the library itself with symbols
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try NuGet symbol server
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1
arguments.description: Try fetching symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Try fetching symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in the backtrace. The tombstone shows frames from `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with a Build ID of `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. 

To symbolicate these frames, I would need:
1. The debug symbols for `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with that specific Build ID
2. A tool like `llvm-symbolizer`, `addr2line`, or similar

The Build ID `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be` identifies the exact build of the Mono runtime. This is from a .NET MAUI app, so this would be a specific version of the .NET runtime for Android.
 
Now I'm thinking about how to fetch the debug symbols from Microsoft's symbol servers. Microsoft publishes symbol packages for .NET runtime libraries, and I should be able to retrieve them using the Build ID. The symbol server URL structure varies depending on whether we're dealing with Windows PDB files or ELF files with Build IDs, so I need to figure out the correct endpoint for Linux/Android native libraries.

I have the Build ID `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be` for the libmonosgen-2.0.so file, and I'm trying to construct the proper symbol server URL to download the debug symbols. The format for ELF files typically includes the Build ID in a specific way, so I'm testing different URL patterns to see which one works.

I'm realizing this might be synthetic test data with a fabricated Build ID, but I should still attempt to fetch the symbols from Microsoft's symbol server to see if anything comes back. The most straightforward approach would be to use the `dotnet-symbol` tool, which can handle downloading symbols by Build ID directly. I realize this is a test file with fabricated data—the typo in the abort message and the intentional crash message make that clear—so the Build ID probably won't map to real symbols. Still, I'll check what symbolication tools are available and attempt to fetch from the Microsoft symbol server, then explain what would be needed if that doesn't work.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line dotnet 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5; echo "---"; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i binutils | head -5

# Try fetching debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using the Build ID
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"

# Try the ELF debug symbol endpoint
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try the library itself with symbols
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try NuGet symbol server
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1
